name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: Ancient rock fortress and palace ruins dating back to 5th century AD. UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring remarkable frescoes, mirror wall, and lion's gate. Summit offers panoramic views of surrounding forests and countryside.
best_time_to_visit: January to April, August to September
entry_fee: USD 30 for foreigners, LKR 100 for locals

name: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
description: Sacred Buddhist temple housing tooth relic of Buddha. Located in royal palace complex. Important pilgrimage site with daily religious ceremonies and annual Esala Perahera festival.
entry_fee: LKR 2000 for foreigners, LKR 500 for locals

name: Yala National Park
description: Most visited and second largest national park in Sri Lanka. Highest leopard density in the world. Home to elephants, sloth bears, crocodiles, and over 200 bird species.
entry_fee: USD 25-30 per person plus jeep charges
duration: Half day or full day safari

name: Galle Fort
description: 17th century Dutch fort and UNESCO World Heritage Site. Well-preserved colonial architecture with cobblestone streets, boutique hotels, cafes, and museums. Surrounded by ocean on three sides.

name: Ella
description: Scenic hill country town famous for tea plantations, hiking trails, and stunning mountain views. Popular backpacker destination with laid-back atmosphere.
best_time_to_visit: January to March, July to September

name: Mirissa Beach
description: Crescent-shaped beach famous for whale watching, surfing, and beach parties. Palm-fringed coastline with excellent seafood restaurants and nightlife.
best_time_to_visit: November to April for whale watching

name: Anuradhapura Ancient City
description: Ancient capital with sacred Buddhist sites over 2000 years old. UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring massive stupas, ancient pools, and Sri Maha Bodhi tree.

name: Polonnaruwa Ancient Kingdom
description: Medieval capital of Sri Lanka with well-preserved ruins. UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring royal palace, Gal Vihara rock sculptures, and ancient irrigation systems.

name: Nuwara Eliya
description: Scenic hill station known as 'Little England' with colonial architecture, tea plantations, and cool climate. Surrounded by mountains, valleys, and waterfalls.

name: Horton Plains National Park
description: Highland plateau with unique biodiversity and stunning landscapes. Home to World's End cliff (880m drop) and Baker's Falls. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Dambulla Cave Temple
description: Largest and best-preserved cave temple complex in Sri Lanka. Five caves containing 153 Buddha statues, ancient murals covering 2,100 square meters. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Udawalawe National Park
description: Important habitat for Sri Lankan elephants with population of over 500. Created around Udawalawe Reservoir with grasslands and scrub jungle. Excellent for wildlife photography.
entry_fee: USD 15-20 per person plus jeep charges

name: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
description: Last viable rainforest in Sri Lanka with extraordinary biodiversity. UNESCO World Heritage Site and Biosphere Reserve. Over 60% endemic species of trees and wildlife.
best_time_to_visit: January to April, August to September

name: Arugam Bay
description: World-renowned surfing destination with consistent waves and laid-back beach culture. Crescent-shaped bay with golden sand beaches and excellent surf points.

name: Adam's Peak (Sri Pada)
description: Sacred mountain pilgrimage site revered by multiple religions. Features sacred footprint at summit. Popular for sunrise climbs with spectacular views.

name: Trincomalee
description: Natural deep-water harbor with pristine beaches, coral reefs, and historical sites. Famous for whale watching, diving, and Koneswaram Temple perched on cliff.

name: Bentota Beach
description: Popular beach resort destination with calm waters ideal for water sports. Bentota River meets ocean creating unique ecosystem. Luxury hotels and water activity centers.

name: Hikkaduwa
description: Vibrant beach town famous for coral reefs, sea turtles, and surfing. Coral sanctuary with glass-bottom boat tours. Active nightlife and beachfront restaurants.

name: Jaffna
description: Cultural capital of Sri Lankan Tamil community with distinct cuisine, architecture, and traditions. Ancient temples, Dutch fort, and unique cultural experiences.

name: Wilpattu National Park
description: Largest national park in Sri Lanka with unique 'willus' (natural lakes). Dense forest and scrubland habitat for leopards, elephants, and sloth bears.
entry_fee: USD 25-30 per person plus jeep charges

name: Nine Arch Bridge
description: Iconic colonial-era railway bridge built entirely of stone and bricks without steel. Set amidst tea plantations and jungle. Popular photography spot especially during train crossings.

name: Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage
description: Conservation center housing orphaned and injured elephants. Famous for elephant bathing sessions in Maha Oya River. Breeding program for Asian elephants.

name: Minneriya National Park
description: Famous for 'The Gathering' - largest meeting of Asian elephants in world (July-September). Ancient Minneriya Tank attracts hundreds of elephants during dry season.
best_time_to_visit: July to September for elephant gathering
entry_fee: USD 25 per person plus jeep charges

name: Negombo
description: Historic fishing town near airport with Dutch colonial heritage. Active fish market, lagoon, and beach resorts. Gateway for many tourists arriving in Sri Lanka.

name: Unawatuna
description: Crescent-shaped bay with calm waters perfect for swimming. Coral reefs for snorkeling and diving. Vibrant backpacker scene with beachfront restaurants and bars.

name: Tangalle
description: Quiet coastal town with pristine beaches and relaxed atmosphere. Turtle nesting sites, lagoons, and unspoiled natural beauty. Less developed than other southern beaches.

name: Knuckles Mountain Range
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with unique biodiversity and dramatic landscapes. Named for resemblance to clenched knuckles. Popular trekking destination with multiple trails.
best_time_to_visit: January to March, August to September

name: Matara
description: Southern coastal city with Dutch fort, beaches, and temples. Important transport hub with mix of colonial heritage and modern Sri Lankan culture.

name: Kalpitiya
description: Emerging destination famous for kitesurfing, dolphin and whale watching. Peninsula with lagoons on one side and ocean on other. Pristine beaches and water sports.
best_time_to_visit: May to October for kitesurfing, November to April for dolphin watching

name: Mihintale
description: Sacred mountain where Buddhism was introduced to Sri Lanka in 3rd century BC. Ancient monastery complex with stupas, caves, and stone inscriptions. Over 1,800 steps to summit.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round, especially June during Poson Poya

name: Pigeon Island National Park
description: Marine national park with best coral reefs in Sri Lanka. Two rocky islets surrounded by clear turquoise waters. Excellent snorkeling and diving with diverse marine life.

name: Ravana Falls
description: Popular waterfall on Colombo-Badulla highway near Ella. Named after legendary King Ravana. Multiple cascades create pool for swimming at base.

name: Kandy Lake
description: Artificial lake in heart of Kandy created by last Sinhalese king. Scenic walking path around lake with views of Temple of Tooth and surrounding hills.

name: Kaudulla National Park
description: Alternative to Minneriya for elephant gathering. Ancient Kaudulla Tank attracts elephants and water birds. Part of elephant corridor connecting several national parks.
entry_fee: USD 25 per person plus jeep charges

name: Weligama Bay
description: Sheltered bay ideal for beginner surfers with consistent gentle waves. Famous for stilt fishermen. Crescent beach with good swimming conditions.

name: Bundala National Park
description: UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and important wetland for migratory waterbirds. Over 197 bird species including flamingos. Elephants, crocodiles, and sea turtles also present.
best_time_to_visit: September to March for migratory birds
entry_fee: USD 15-20 per person plus jeep charges

name: Habarana
description: Central location for accessing Cultural Triangle sites and wildlife parks. Village atmosphere with eco-lodges and safari operators.

name: Peradeniya Royal Botanical Gardens
description: 147-acre botanical garden with over 4,000 species of plants. Famous orchid collection, giant bamboo, and palm avenues. Historic gardens dating to 1821.

name: Pasikuda Beach
description: Shallow bay with calm waters extending 100 meters from shore. Safe swimming and water sports. Pristine white sand beach with turquoise waters.

name: Mulkirigala Rock Temple
description: Ancient rock temple with seven levels reached by stone steps. Cave temples with Buddha statues and murals dating back 2,000 years. Panoramic views from summit.

name: Ritigala Forest Monastery
description: Ancient Buddhist monastery ruins in strict nature reserve. Mysterious forest hermitage with unique architecture. Rich biodiversity with medicinal plants.

name: Koggala Lake
description: Large coastal lake with islands containing temples and cinnamon plantations. Rich birdlife and mangrove ecosystems. Boat tours available.

name: Batticaloa
description: Eastern coastal city with Dutch fort, lagoon, and unique cultural heritage. Famous for singing fish phenomenon. Gateway to eastern beaches.

name: Kumana National Park
description: Important bird sanctuary and extension of Yala National Park. Kumana Villu wetland attracts thousands of waterbirds. Elephants, leopards, and crocodiles also present.
entry_fee: USD 20-25 per person plus jeep charges

name: Yapahuwa Rock Fortress
description: 13th century rock fortress and former capital. Impressive ornamental stairway and stone carvings. Less crowded alternative to Sigiriya.

name: Mannar Island
description: Remote island with unique culture, ancient baobab trees, and bird sanctuaries. Adam's Bridge (Rama Setu) connects to India. Rich marine biodiversity.

name: Ambuluwawa Tower
description: Multi-religious complex on mountain peak with spiral tower offering 360-degree views. Unique architecture combining Buddhist, Hindu, Muslim, and Christian elements.
entry_fee: LKR 100 for locals, higher for foreigners

name: Pidurangala Rock
description: Ancient rock temple with easier climb than Sigiriya. Stunning sunrise views with Sigiriya Rock in foreground. Giant reclining Buddha statue.

name: Lipton's Seat
description: Viewpoint where Sir Thomas Lipton surveyed his tea empire. Panoramic views of southern hill country and plains. Scenic drive through tea plantations.
best_time_to_visit: January to March for clear views

name: Uda Walawe Reservoir
description: Large reservoir within Udawalawe National Park. Scenic water body attracting elephants and waterbirds. Boat rides available in some sections.

name: Kalutara
description: Coastal town famous for large Buddhist stupa (Kalutara Bodhiya) and beaches. Important for manufacturing quality incense. Kalu Ganga river meets ocean here.

name: Gal Oya National Park
description: Only park in Sri Lanka where boat safaris are conducted. Senanayake Samudraya reservoir. Elephants swim between islands. Remote and less visited.
best_time_to_visit: March to July, December to January
entry_fee: USD 20-25 per person plus boat charges

name: Ravana Cave
description: Cave associated with Ramayana epic where King Ravana supposedly hid Princess Sita. Temple inside cave with Buddha statues. Climb offers views of Ella valley.

name: Dondra Head Lighthouse
description: Southernmost point of Sri Lanka with historic lighthouse built in 1889. Tall lighthouse offering coastal views. Near Dondra temple ruins.

name: Ramboda Falls
description: 109-meter high waterfall on Colombo-Nuwara Eliya highway. Series of cascades surrounded by tea plantations. Associated with Ramayana legend.

name: Belilena Cave
description: Prehistoric cave with evidence of human habitation 32,000 years ago. Important archaeological site. Balangoda Man skeletal remains discovered here.

name: Kitulgala
description: Adventure capital of Sri Lanka famous for white-water rafting on Kelani River. Rainforest location where 'Bridge on River Kwai' was filmed. Rich birdlife.

name: Belihuloya
description: Small mountain village with stunning scenery and waterfalls. Gateway to Horton Plains. Ideal for hiking, camping, and nature activities.
best_time_to_visit: January to March, July to September

name: Bambarakanda Falls
description: Tallest waterfall in Sri Lanka at 263 meters (863 feet). Surrounded by pine forests with trekking trail to base. Remote location with pristine natural beauty.
best_time_to_visit: March to May, November to December

name: Madu River
description: Wetland ecosystem with mangroves, islands, and biodiversity. Boat safari through narrow waterways. Cinnamon islands and fish therapy available.

name: Delft Island
description: Remote island accessible by boat from Jaffna. Wild ponies, coral walls, Dutch colonial ruins, and unique culture. Baobab trees and ancient Buddhist ruins.

name: Nainativu Island
description: Sacred island with important Buddhist and Hindu temples. Nagadipa Purana Vihara and Naga Pooshani Amman Temple. Pilgrimage site for both religions.

name: Dickwella Beach
description: Quiet beach with natural rock pools and nearby Wewurukannala Temple. Less crowded southern beach with authentic fishing village atmosphere.

name: Hoo-maniya Blowhole
description: Natural blowhole where waves force water up to 25 meters high through rock formation. Second largest blowhole in world. Impressive during rough seas.
best_time_to_visit: May to September for best displays

name: Kithulgala Rain Forest
description: Dense tropical rainforest with high biodiversity. Endemic bird species and mammals. Part of Sinharaja buffer zone with trekking opportunities.
best_time_to_visit: January to April, August to September

name: Casuarina Beach (Karainagar)
description: Beautiful white sand beach lined with casuarina trees. Shallow clear waters ideal for swimming. Remote northern beach with peaceful atmosphere.

name: Maduru Oya National Park
description: Remote national park protecting ancient reservoirs and archaeological sites. Elephants, deer, and Vedda settlements. Less visited wilderness area.

name: Wasgamuwa National Park
description: National park with large elephant herds and diverse wildlife. Mix of grassland, forest, and wetland habitats. Less crowded alternative to popular parks.
entry_fee: USD 20 per person plus jeep charges

name: Nalanda Gedige
description: Unique stone building combining Hindu and Buddhist architecture. Ancient structure possibly from 8th-10th century. Only complete stone building from ancient era.

name: Aluvihare Rock Temple
description: Historic temple where Buddhist scriptures were first written down on palm leaves. Cave temple with important religious significance. Ancient library ruins.

name: Buduruwagala
description: Ancient Buddhist rock sculptures carved into cliff face. Seven statues including 16-meter tall Buddha, largest in Sri Lanka. Dating from 9th-10th century.

name: Rekawa Beach
description: Important sea turtle nesting site for all five species found in Sri Lanka. Night turtle watching tours available. Pristine beach with conservation focus.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round for turtles, November-April for beach

name: Ussangoda National Park
description: Unique red-soil coastal plateau with unusual vegetation. Meteorite impact site theory. Sacred site with archaeological findings. Stunning coastal views.

name: Kalametiya Bird Sanctuary
description: Coastal lagoon and wetland bird sanctuary. Over 150 bird species including migratory and resident waterbirds. Mangroves and salt marshes.

name: Keerimalai Springs
description: Natural spring-fed pool with religious significance. Hindu temple nearby. Freshwater springs right by the ocean. Local pilgrimage site.

name: Talaimannar
description: Northwestern coastal town at tip of Mannar Island. Adam's Bridge begins here. Historic ferry terminal and lighthouse. Pristine beaches.

name: Bogoda Wooden Bridge
description: Ancient wooden cantilever bridge with temple roof. Only remaining covered wooden bridge in Sri Lanka. Dating back to 16th century over Gallanda Oya.

name: Dunhinda Falls
description: 63-meter high waterfall creating misty spray. Name means 'smoky waterfall' in Sinhala. Scenic forest walk to reach falls. Popular among locals.

name: Devon Falls
description: 97-meter waterfall visible from main road between Hatton and Nuwara Eliya. Named after English coffee planter. Surrounded by tea estates.

name: St. Clair's Falls
description: Known as 'Little Niagara of Sri Lanka'. Twin cascades totaling 80 meters. Visible from main road with viewing areas. Set among tea plantations.

name: Dhowa Rock Temple
description: Ancient rock temple with unfinished Buddha sculpture carved into rock face. Cave temple with 18th century murals. Believed to date from 1st century BC.

name: Embekke Devalaya
description: 14th century temple famous for intricate wood carvings. Hundreds of carved wooden pillars with unique designs. Important example of Kandyan architecture.

name: Lankatilaka Temple
description: Ancient temple built on rock outcrop with Sinhalese and South Indian architectural features. Dating from 14th century. Stunning valley views.

name: Gadaladeniya Temple
description: 14th century stone temple with South Indian architectural influence. Built on rock with panoramic views. Important ancient Buddhist temple.

name: Seenigama Devalaya
description: Hindu temple on small island connected by bridge to coast. Colorful architecture visible from Colombo-Galle road. Sacred to both Buddhists and Hindus.

name: Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery
description: Sea turtle conservation project protecting eggs and hatchlings. Five turtle species. Educational tours and release programs. Active conservation efforts.

name: Barberyn Island (Beruwala)
description: Small island with historic lighthouse built in 1889. Island monastery and retreat center. Accessible by boat. Peaceful meditation destination.

name: Brief Garden
description: Landscaped garden and former home of artist Bevis Bawa. Sculpture garden with art collection. Tropical paradise with unique design aesthetics.

name: Lunuganga Estate
description: Garden estate of architect Geoffrey Bawa. Beautiful landscape design with sculptures and water features. Important example of tropical modernism.

name: Dediy agala (Virgin White Rock)
description: White limestone cliff formation near Aranayake. Popular viewpoint with panoramic views. Easier alternative to other rock climbs. Sunset views.

name: Pidurutalagala
description: Highest mountain in Sri Lanka at 2,524 meters (8,281 feet). Communication tower at summit. Restricted area requiring permission. Surrounded by tea estates.

name: Hakgala Botanical Gardens
description: Second largest botanical garden in Sri Lanka. Alpine plants, roses, orchids. Associated with Ramayana legend. Cool climate garden at 1,745 meters elevation.

name: Seetha Amman Temple
description: Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Sita from Ramayana epic. Believed site where Sita was held captive. Sacred footprints on rock. Pilgrimage site.

name: Kelaniya Raja Maha Vihara
description: Ancient Buddhist temple near Colombo visited by Buddha. Beautiful murals and architecture. Important pilgrimage site with annual Duruthu Perahera festival.

name: Gangaramaya Temple
description: Famous Buddhist temple in Colombo with museum, library, and residential halls. Mix of modern and traditional architecture. Hosts annual Navam Perahera.

name: Viharamahadevi Park
description: Largest park in Colombo with children's play areas, walking paths, and giant Buddha statue. Historical colonial park formerly named Victoria Park.

name: National Museum Colombo
description: Largest museum in Sri Lanka with extensive collection of artifacts, royal regalia, and historical exhibits. Colonial-era building housing national treasures.

name: Galle Face Green
description: Historic urban park along Colombo coast. Popular evening hangout for locals. Street food vendors, kite flying, and ocean views. Colonial-era promenade.

name: Independence Square
description: National monument commemorating Sri Lanka's independence from British rule. Independence Memorial Hall with Kandyan architecture. Popular jogging area.

name: Pettah Market
description: Bustling historic market area with narrow streets and diverse goods. Textiles, spices, electronics, and local products. Authentic local shopping experience.

name: Colombo Fort
description: Historic colonial district with financial and commercial buildings. Mix of old Dutch and British colonial architecture. Central business district.

name: Beira Lake
description: Large lake in heart of Colombo city. Seema Malaka temple on water, boat rides, and walking paths. Popular for evening strolls and recreation.

name: Lotus Tower
description: Tallest self-supported structure in South Asia at 350 meters. Observation deck with panoramic Colombo views. Modern architectural landmark shaped like lotus.

name: Muthurajawela Marsh
description: Coastal wetland sanctuary near Negombo. Rich biodiversity with mangroves, waterbirds, and wildlife. Boat safari tours through marshland channels.

name: Aukana Buddha Statue
description: Giant standing Buddha statue carved from granite rock. 12 meters tall dating from 5th century. One of finest ancient statues in Sri Lanka. Serene facial expression.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round, early morning for best light

name: Avukana Buddha Statue
description: Ancient standing Buddha statue similar to Aukana. Carved from rock face in 5th century. Alternative name 'Aukana'. Impressive craftsmanship.

name: Isurumuniya Temple
description: Rock temple in Anuradhapura with famous Isurumuniya Lovers sculpture. Ancient pool and cave temple. Important archaeological and artistic site.

name: Abhayagiri Dagoba
description: Ancient Buddhist monastery complex and massive stupa in Anuradhapura. Once 115 meters tall. Extensive ruins of monastery buildings and sculptures.

name: Ruwanwelisaya
description: Great white stupa in Anuradhapura built by King Dutugemunu. Major pilgrimage site. Elephant wall surrounding base. Important Buddhist monument.

name: Sri Maha Bodhi
description: Sacred fig tree grown from cutting of original Bodhi tree under which Buddha attained enlightenment. Over 2,000 years old. Most sacred Buddhist site in Sri Lanka.

name: Little Adam's Peak
description: Easy hiking trail near Ella with stunning 360-degree views. Named for resemblance to Adam's Peak. Popular sunrise and sunset spot. Takes 30-45 minutes to climb.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round, early morning or late afternoon

name: Ella Rock
description: Challenging trek through tea plantations and forest to rocky summit. Panoramic views of Ella Gap and surrounding valleys. Takes 2-3 hours one way.

name: Lovers Leap Waterfall (Ella)
description: Waterfall near Ella with tragic love story legend. Accessible via short walk. Good views from tea estate. Also known as Ella Falls.

name: Demodara Nine Arch Bridge (alternate view)
description: Best viewing spots and different angles of iconic Nine Arch Bridge. Walking paths through tea plantations. Train schedule viewing opportunities.

name: Demodara Railway Station Loop
description: Unique railway engineering where track loops under itself through tunnel. Only place in Sri Lanka with such design. Near Nine Arch Bridge.

name: Bolgoda Lake
description: Coastal district capital with beaches and war memorials. Historically significant location. Coastal beauty and fishing communities.

name: Point Pedro
description: Northernmost point of Sri Lanka. Lighthouse and coastal scenery. Fishing village atmosphere.

name: Nallur Kandaswamy Temple
description: Most important Hindu temple in Jaffna. Dedicated to Lord Murugan. Grand annual festival with chariot processions.

name: Jaffna Fort
description: Dutch colonial fort with Portuguese origins. Largest fort in Sri Lanka. Recently restored with museum and rampart walks.

name: Jaffna Public Library
description: Historic library rebuilt after 1981 burning. Symbol of Tamil culture and knowledge. Beautiful Indo-Saracenic architecture.

name: Matale Town
description: District capital famous for spice gardens. Hindu temples and colonial heritage. Gateway to Cultural Triangle.

name: Sembuwatta Lake
description: Natural mountain lake surrounded by tea estates and pine forests. Swimming, picnicking, and boat rides available.

name: Riverston
description: Scenic plateau with Mini World's End cliff viewpoint. Cloud forests and biodiversity. Part of Knuckles buffer zone.

name: Gampola
description: Ancient capital of Sri Lanka in 14th century. Historical temples and colonial buildings. Important railway junction.

name: Mahiyanganaya
description: Ancient town with temple believed visited by Buddha. Gateway to Maduru Oya Park. Vedda cultural center.

name: Sorabora Wewa
description: Ancient reservoir with unique sluice design. Engineering marvel from 2nd century. Legend of giant Bulatha involvement.

name: Victoria Dam & Reservoir
description: Largest hydroelectric dam in Sri Lanka. Scenic reservoir surrounded by mountains. Victoria Park and gardens nearby.

name: Teldeniya
description: Small town near Victoria Dam with river views. Base for exploring Mahaweli development. Agricultural area.

name: Kotmale Reservoir
description: Hydroelectric reservoir in beautiful mountain setting. Part of Mahaweli development. Scenic mountain backdrop.

name: Castlereagh Reservoir
description: Picturesque reservoir surrounded by tea estates. Cool climate and misty conditions. Popular picnic spot.

name: Hatton
description: Tea estate town and base for Adam's Peak climb. Tamil tea workers' culture. Cool mountain climate.

name: Maskeliya
description: Tea plantation area near Adam's Peak base. Multiple reservoirs including Canyon Falls viewpoint. Scenic mountain drives.

name: Laxapana Falls
description: 126-meter waterfall feeding hydroelectric power station. Seventh tallest in Sri Lanka. Misty mountain setting.

name: Talawakelle
description: Tea plantation town between Hatton and Nuwara Eliya. Colonial bungalows and tea factories. Cool climate.

name: Kotagala
description: Small tea estate town with beautiful scenery. Aberdeen Falls nearby. Less touristy hill country location.

name: Aberdeen Falls
description: 98-meter waterfall requiring moderate trek through tea estates. Less visited than other major falls. Natural pool at base.

name: Nanu Oya
description: Railway station serving Nuwara Eliya. Scenic train journey endpoint from Kandy. Cool climate junction town.

name: Ambewela
description: Highland plateau with dairy farms and grasslands. New Zealand-like landscape. Ambewela Farm produces milk for island.

name: Pattipola
description: Highest railway station in Sri Lanka at 1,898 meters. Scenic mountain plateau. Cold climate and misty conditions.

name: Ohiya
description: Small mountain village and trail start for Horton Plains trek. Beautiful highland scenery. Remote and peaceful.

name: Kalupahana
description: Remote village near Bambarakanda Falls. Highland farming community. Base for waterfall treks.

name: Koslanda
description: Small village near Diyaluma Falls. Tea estates and rural life. Base for waterfall exploration.

name: Passara
description: Small town near Namunukula mountain range. Agricultural center with scenic surroundings. Gateway to hiking areas.

name: Namunukula Mountain Range
description: Mountain range with trekking trails and panoramic views. Less visited than other peaks. Rich biodiversity.

name: Hambantota Town
description: District capital with new port and airport. Developing city with modern infrastructure. Gateway to southern wildlife parks.

name: Tissamaharama
description: Ancient capital and gateway to Yala. Tissa Wewa reservoir and Buddhist dagobas. Safari base town.

name: Kataragama
description: Sacred multi-religious pilgrimage town. Hindu, Buddhist, and indigenous worship. Major annual Esala festival.

name: Kirinda
description: Coastal village with rock temple overlooking ocean. Viharaya Temple and Queen Viharamahadevi legend. Beach and surf spot.

name: Ambalantota
description: Small town between Tangalle and Hambantota. Agricultural center and curd production. Rural Sri Lankan life.

name: Weerawila
description: Town with large bird sanctuary on reservoir. Migratory and resident waterbirds. Fishing community.

name: Beliatta
description: Small agricultural town near Walawe River. Birthplace of former president. Rural southern character.

name: Angunakolapelessa
description: Junction town connecting south to hill country. Agricultural area with paddy fields. Rest stop on travels.

name: Embilipitiya
description: Town gateway to Udawalawe Park. Agricultural center. Transit point between south and hill country.

name: Balangoda
description: Hill country town with prehistoric significance. Balangoda Man discovery site. Tea and rubber estates.

name: Samanalawewa Reservoir
description: Beautiful mountain reservoir for hydroelectricity. Scenic location on Adam's Peak approach. Fishing and boating.

name: Pelmadulla
description: Junction town on Colombo-Ratnapura road. Agricultural market center. Transit point for travelers.

name: Bolgoda Lake
description: Largest lake in Western Province with water sports facilities. Popular for jet skiing, kayaking, and boat rides. Mangrove ecosystems and birdlife.

name: Mount Lavinia Beach
description: Popular beach in Colombo suburbs with colonial heritage. Mount Lavinia Hotel overlooks beach. Swimming, dining, and sunset views.

name: Dehiwala Zoo
description: National Zoological Gardens with diverse animal collection. Elephant shows, aquarium, and butterfly garden. Popular family destination.

name: Diyaluma Falls
description: Second highest waterfall in Sri Lanka at 220 meters. Natural infinity pools at top with stunning views. Popular swimming spot.

name: Hanthana Mountain Range
description: Seven peaks near Kandy offering various hiking trails. Panoramic views of Kandy city and surrounding valleys. Rich biodiversity.
best_time_to_visit: January to March, July to September

name: Udawattakele Forest Reserve
description: Historic forest reserve on hill ridge overlooking Kandy. Royal forest with walking trails, diverse wildlife, and ancient trees.

name: Kurunegala
description: District capital with giant rock formations. Ethagala (Elephant Rock) dominates skyline. Buddhist temples and colonial buildings.

name: Ethagala (Elephant Rock)
description: Massive rock formation resembling elephant. Dominates Kurunegala skyline. Short hike to viewpoint offers city panorama.

name: Dambadeniya Ancient Kingdom
description: Medieval capital of Sri Lanka in 13th century. Ruins of royal palace, tooth relic temple, and ancient reservoirs.

name: Panduwasnuwara
description: Ancient kingdom ruins with moat and palace remains. 12th century capital. Well-preserved citadel and tank system.

name: Chilaw
description: Coastal town famous for Munneswaram Temple and lagoon. Fishing industry center. Gateway to Kalpitiya.

name: Munneswaram Temple
description: Ancient Hindu temple complex with five temples. Important pilgrimage site. Associated with Ramayana legend. Annual festivals.

name: Anamaduwa
description: Agricultural town known for pottery and traditional crafts. Ancient reservoirs and rural village experience.

name: Ratnapura Gem Mines
description: Gem mining capital of Sri Lanka. Visit working gem pits, museums, and trading centers. Famous for sapphires and rubies.
best_time_to_visit: Year-round, dry season better for mines

name: Maha Saman Devalaya
description: Important Buddhist-Hindu temple dedicated to deity Saman. Gateway to Adam's Peak pilgrimage. Annual Esala Perahera festival.

name: Bopath Ella Falls
description: Waterfall shaped like Bo leaf (sacred fig). 30 meters high with natural pool. Easy access from road. Legend of cursed treasure.

name: Kegalle Town
description: District capital on Colombo-Kandy highway. Gateway to Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage. Rubber and coconut plantation center.

name: Aranayake
description: Hill country town with tea estates and spice gardens. Base for Dediyagala rock climb. Cool climate and scenic landscapes.

name: Monaragala
description: District capital and gateway to eastern national parks. Agricultural center with ancient Buddhist sites. Peaceful rural town.

name: Wellawaya
description: Junction town connecting hill country to southern plains. Base for visiting Buduruwagala. Rice farming area.

name: Badulla Town
description: District capital and end of scenic train line from Colombo. Historic town with temples and colonial buildings. Hill country commercial center.

name: Muthiyangana Raja Maha Vihara
description: Ancient Buddhist temple believed visited by Buddha. Important pilgrimage site in Badulla. Annual Esala Perahera festival.

name: Bandarawela
description: Charming hill station with cool climate and colonial architecture. Tea estates and scenic railway. Slower pace than Ella or Nuwara Eliya.

name: Haputale
description: Small hill town with dramatic escarpment views. Lipton's Seat viewpoint nearby. Tea estate workers' culture and Tamil heritage.

name: Adisham Monastery
description: Benedictine monastery in English country house style. Built in 1931, now occupied by monks. Beautiful gardens and architecture.

name: Ampara
description: District capital with diverse ethnic community. Agricultural hub for rice cultivation. Gateway to eastern national parks.

name: Lahugala National Park
description: Small national park known for elephant population. Lahugala Tank attracts elephants year-round. Easy wildlife viewing from roadside.

name: Pottuvil
description: Coastal village near Arugam Bay. Pottuvil Point surf break and lagoon. Ancient Muhudu Maha Vihara temple nearby.

name: Kudumbigala Monastery
description: Ancient forest hermitage on rocky outcrop. Panoramic views of surrounding wilderness. Active meditation center with resident monks.

name: Panama
description: Remote fishing village with pristine beaches. Gateway to Kumana National Park. Peaceful atmosphere and beautiful sunrises.

name: Kalkudah Beach
description: Beautiful beach with calm shallow waters extending far from shore. Sister beach to Pasikuda. Safe swimming and water sports.

name: Kallady Beach
description: Beach in Batticaloa town with Lady Manning Bridge landmark. Singing fish phenomenon location. Urban beach with facilities.

name: Nilaveli Beach
description: Long stretch of white sand beach with turquoise waters. Gateway to Pigeon Island. Calm conditions ideal for swimming.

name: Uppuveli Beach
description: Quieter beach near Trincomalee with clear waters. Good for swimming and diving. Dutch Bay Beach nearby.

name: Koneswaram Temple
description: Ancient Hindu temple on Swami Rock cliff overlooking ocean. One of five Ishwarams of Shiva. Dramatic coastal location with history.

name: Fort Frederick
description: Portuguese fort later occupied by Dutch and British. Still active military area. Contains Koneswaram Temple within grounds.

name: Vavuniya
description: Gateway to Northern Province. Transport hub connecting north to rest of country. Agricultural market town.

name: Madhu Church
description: Important Catholic pilgrimage site. Our Lady of Madhu shrine. Peaceful forest setting. Annual feast attracts thousands.

name: Kilinochchi
description: District capital rebuilding after civil war. Historical significance. War memorials and new development.
